basics.name: Sichi Yan
basics.summary: M.S. candidate in Mechanical Engineering at Shanghai Jiao Tong University (SJTU). Research: legged-robot reinforcement learning, morphology–control co-design, and real-robot deployment. GPA 3.79/4.0 (rank 26/274). Seeking RA positions and PhD programs in robotics.

work.summary: Working on reinforcement-learning-based locomotion control for robotic systems.
Developed an end-to-end ROS2-based inference pipeline integrating a LoRA-adapted vision–language model with RGB-D sensing for near–real-time robotic scene understanding.
highlights: End-to-end pipeline: RGB-D data collection, LoRA fine-tuning of Qwen2.5-VL via Qwen2-VL-Finetune, and ROS2 service-node deployment.
Trigger-based ROS2 inference for near–real-time test-tube state recognition in laboratory automation.
Covered dataset generation, model adaptation, offline validation, and on-line deployment on Linux.

publications.name: Human2Nav: Learning Crowd Navigation from Human Videos across Robots via Feasibility-Guided Flow Matching
publications.publisher: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA 2026)
publications.summary: A data-efficient framework learning robot crowd navigation policies directly from passive human videos via BEV representation and conditional flow matching. Test-time feasibility-guided inference adapts trajectories to heterogeneous robot kinematics without retraining. Deployed on differential-drive and quadrupedal robots across four crowd scenarios.

publications.name: CALA: Co-design with Accelerated Learning for Terrain-Specific Adaptive Locomotion
publications.publisher: IEEE International Conference on Real-time Computing and Robotics (RCAR 2026)
publications.summary: A hierarchical bi-level co-design framework for terrain-specific adaptive locomotion, combining CMA-ES morphology search with rapid PPO fine-tuning. Validated on Unitree Go2 and Ant platforms across six terrain–platform combinations.

portfolio.name: ROS2 VLM Inference
portfolio.description: End-to-end pipeline for robotic scene understanding using a LoRA-adapted vision–language model in a ROS2 service node with RGB-D sensing, for laboratory automation.
